# Generated from .github/targets.toml by `mise run ci:generate`.
# Do not edit this file directly; edit the manifest and regenerate.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false

            test_native: true
            java_ffm: true
            java_jni: true
            dotnet: true
            kotlin_native: false
            rust: true
            swift: false
            zig: true
            lwjgl_example: false
            rust_example: true
            swift_example: false
            zig_example: true
            zig_readback: true
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            test_native: true
            java_ffm: true

            dotnet: true

            java_jni: true
            dotnet: true
            kotlin_native: true
            rust: true
            swift: false
            zig: true
            lwjgl_example: false
            rust_example: true
            swift_example: false
            zig_example: true
            zig_readback: true
          - mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-latest
            test_native: true
            java_ffm: true
            java_jni: true
            dotnet: true
            kotlin_native: true
            rust: true
            swift: false
            zig: true
            lwjgl_example: true

            zig_example: true

          - mise_env: macos-arm64-metal

            test_native: true
            java_ffm: true
            java_jni: true
            dotnet: true
            kotlin_native: true
            rust: true
            swift: true
            zig: true
            lwjgl_example: false
            rust_example: true
            swift_example: true
            zig_example: true
            zig_readback: true
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-vulkan
            runner: macos-latest
            test_native: true
            java_ffm: true
            java_jni: true
            dotnet: true
            kotlin_native: true
            rust: true

          - mise_env: macos-arm64-egl
            runner: macos-latest
            test_native: true

            java_jni: false

          - mise_env: ios-arm64-metal

            zig_example: false

          - mise_env: ios-simulator-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
            test_native: false
            java_ffm: false
            java_jni: false

            kotlin_native: false
            rust: false

            zig_example: false

          - mise_env: windows-x64-vulkan
            runner: windows-2022
            test_native: true
            java_ffm: true
            java_jni: true

            kotlin_native: false

            swift: false
            zig: true

            swift_example: false
            zig_example: true

            runner: windows-2022
            test_native: true
            java_ffm: true
            java_jni: true
            dotnet: true

            rust_example: true
            swift_example: false
            zig_example: true
            zig_readback: true
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ !matrix.test_native }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ matrix.test_native }}
        run: mise run test
      - name: Build Java FFM binding
        if: ${{ matrix.java_ffm }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build
      - name: Build Java JNI binding
        if: ${{ matrix.java_jni }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-jni:build
      - name: Test .NET binding
        if: ${{ matrix.dotnet }}
        run: mise run //bindings/dotnet:test
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
        if: ${{ matrix.kotlin_native }}
        run: mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build
      - name: Test Rust binding
        if: ${{ matrix.rust }}
        run: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
      - name: Test Swift binding
        if: ${{ matrix.swift }}
        run: mise run //bindings/swift:test
      - name: Test Zig binding
        if: ${{ matrix.zig }}
        run: mise run //bindings/zig:ci
      - name: Build LWJGL example
        if: ${{ matrix.lwjgl_example }}
        run: mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build
      - name: Test Rust example
        if: ${{ matrix.rust_example }}
        run: mise run //examples/rust-map:ci
      - name: Build Swift example
        if: ${{ matrix.swift_example }}
        run: mise run //examples/swift-map:build
      - name: Build Zig example
        if: ${{ matrix.zig_example }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-map:build
      - name: Run Zig readback example
        if: ${{ matrix.zig_readback }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true

part.text:
The generated workflow looks good. Now I need to update mise.toml. Let me add the tasks.

I need to think about where to place the sync check in `ci:lint`. The current `ci:lint`:
1. Warms dprint cache
2. Runs `hk fix --all` (formats and lints)
3. `git update-index`
4. Checks git diff for unstaged changes
5. Checks git diff --cached for staged changes

I should add the sync check. The best place is probably after `hk fix --all` but before the git diff checks. Or maybe before `hk fix --all` so it fails fast. But if dprint would reformat the generated workflow, I want dprint to run first.

Actually, the generator output should already be dprint-compatible. But to be safe, I'll run the sync check after `hk fix --all`. That way, if dprint changes the generated file, the sync check will catch that the generator needs to be re-run (or the generator output should be fixed).

Wait, but `hk fix --all` will stage any changes it makes. Then the git diff check will fail if there are changes. The sync check should also fail if the workflow is out of sync.

I think the order should be:
1. dprint cache warm
2. hk fix --all
3. ci:check-sync (generator sync)
4. git update-index
5. git diff checks

If the generator is out of sync, `hk fix --all` might reformat the generated file but the sync check will still fail because it regenerates and compares.

Actually, the sync check regenerates to a temp/output and compares. If dprint reformatted the file, the regenerated output won't match the formatted file, and the check will fail. That's the desired behavior - the generator output should already match the formatted file.

But maybe dprint doesn't change the YAML at all. I hope so.

Let me add the tasks.

84: ## How Tools Fit Together
85: 
86: This repository spans native code, language bindings, examples, tests, and
87: documentation. Each tool owns the layer where it has the clearest dependency
88: model.
89: 
90: Project-managed dependency state keeps builds reproducible. Mise supplies
91: repository tools, owns task execution, and exports the environment consumed by
92: build systems, bindings, and examples. Pixi currently supplies desktop native
93: libraries from [`conda-forge`](https://conda-forge.org/). Platform SDKs such as
94: Xcode, Visual Studio, Linux compiler packages, and the Android SDK remain host
95: toolchain inputs.
96: 
97: [`mise`](https://mise.jdx.dev/) is the contributor entrypoint. It pins top-level
98: tools, installs Git hooks, selects backend variants, and runs repository tasks.
99: Use `mise run ...` for common workflows: build, test, check, fix, and examples.
100: Mise also provides ecosystem entrypoints such as Pixi, Zig, Python, uv, Node,
101: and pnpm.
102: 
103: [`pixi`](https://pixi.sh/) installs the desktop native libraries used by local
104: builds. CMake, Ninja, pkg-config, shader tools, Doxygen, clang-format, and
105: clang-tidy are pinned by mise. Host platform toolchains provide C and C++
106: compilers, and CMake builds the native C/C++ library.
107: 
108: Language package managers own dependencies inside their ecosystems. For example,
109: `uv` owns Python package dependencies, `pnpm` owns Node package dependencies,
110: Gradle owns Java and Kotlin dependencies, and Cargo owns Rust dependencies.
111: Language-specific formatters, linters, analyzers, test frameworks, and code
112: generators usually live with the language package graph they serve.
113: 
114: [`hk`](https://github.com/jdx/hk) orchestrates repository checks for pre-commit,
115: `mise run check`, and `mise run fix`. [`dprint`](https://dprint.dev/) owns
116: repository-wide formatting defaults.
117: 
118: [Astro](https://astro.build/) and [Starlight](https://starlight.astro.build/)

120: `docs/public/reference/` before each docs build.
121:

123:

125: `mise run test` command builds the native library, runs the direct Zig C API
126: suite, and runs the public Zig binding suite for supported host variants.
127: 
128: Use examples for demos and behavior that needs manual validation, such as visual
129: output, interactive input, or host graphics integration.
130: 
131: Keep examples small. This repository includes low-level language bindings and
132: focused integration examples. Full application SDKs live outside this
133: repository.

display.text:
## How Tools Fit Together

This repository spans native code, language bindings, examples, tests, and
documentation. Each tool owns the layer where it has the clearest dependency
model.

Project-managed dependency state keeps builds reproducible. Mise supplies
repository tools, owns task execution, and exports the environment consumed by
build systems, bindings, and examples. Pixi currently supplies desktop native
libraries from [`conda-forge`](https://conda-forge.org/). Platform SDKs such as
Xcode, Visual Studio, Linux compiler packages, and the Android SDK remain host
toolchain inputs.

[`mise`](https://mise.jdx.dev/) is the contributor entrypoint. It pins top-level
tools, installs Git hooks, selects backend variants, and runs repository tasks.
Use `mise run ...` for common workflows: build, test, check, fix, and examples.
Mise also provides ecosystem entrypoints such as Pixi, Zig, Python, uv, Node,
and pnpm.

[`pixi`](https://pixi.sh/) installs the desktop native libraries used by local
builds. CMake, Ninja, pkg-config, shader tools, Doxygen, clang-format, and
clang-tidy are pinned by mise. Host platform toolchains provide C and C++
compilers, and CMake builds the native C/C++ library.

Language package managers own dependencies inside their ecosystems. For example,
`uv` owns Python package dependencies, `pnpm` owns Node package dependencies,
Gradle owns Java and Kotlin dependencies, and Cargo owns Rust dependencies.
Language-specific formatters, linters, analyzers, test frameworks, and code
generators usually live with the language package graph they serve.

[`hk`](https://github.com/jdx/hk) orchestrates repository checks for pre-commit,
`mise run check`, and `mise run fix`. [`dprint`](https://dprint.dev/) owns
repository-wide formatting defaults.

[Astro](https://astro.build/) and [Starlight](https://starlight.astro.build/)
build the documentation site. Generated API reference HTML is installed into
`docs/public/reference/` before each docs build.

## Tests And Examples

Every feature needs automated CI coverage when practical. The root
`mise run test` command builds the native library, runs the direct Zig C API
suite, and runs the public Zig binding suite for supported host variants.

Use examples for demos and behavior that needs manual validation, such as visual
output, interactive input, or host graphics integration.

Keep examples small. This repository includes low-level language bindings and
focused integration examples. Full application SDKs live outside this
repository.

input.newString:
[Astro](https://astro.build/) and [Starlight](https://starlight.astro.build/)
build the documentation site. Generated API reference HTML is installed into
`docs/public/reference/` before each docs build.

## CI Target Support Policy

The CI variant matrix is declared in `.github/targets.toml` and generated into
`.github/workflows/ci.yml`. This keeps the target support policy in one
place and prevents the checked-in workflow from drifting.

When you change the supported targets, runners, or per-target tests, edit the
manifest and regenerate the workflow:

```bash
mise run ci:generate
```

`mise run ci:lint` enforces that the generated workflow stays in sync with the
manifest, and `mise run ci:test-generator` runs the generator's own tests.

## Tests And Examples

with path.open("rb") as file:
        data = tomllib.load(file)

    steps = [Step(**step) for step in data.get("step", [])]
    targets = [
        Target(
            name=target["name"],
            runner=target["runner"],
            build_only=target["build_only"],
            capabilities=frozenset(target.get("capabilities", [])),
            notes=target.get("notes"),
        )
        for target in data.get("target", [])
    ]

    _validate_policy(steps, targets)

    return Policy(steps=steps, targets=targets)
input.newString:
with path.open("rb") as file:
        data = tomllib.load(file)

    steps = [
        Step(
            name=str(step["name"]),
            task=str(step["task"]),
            always=bool(step.get("always", False)),
            build_only=bool(step.get("build_only", False)),
            test_only=bool(step.get("test_only", False)),
            capability=str(step["capability"]) if step.get("capability") else None,
        )
        for step in data.get("step", [])
    ]
    targets = [
        Target(
            name=str(target["name"]),
            runner=str(target["runner"]),
            build_only=bool(target["build_only"]),
            capabilities=frozenset(str(c) for c in target.get("capabilities", [])),
            notes=str(target["notes"]) if target.get("notes") else None,
        )
        for target in data.get("target", [])
    ]

    _validate_policy(steps, targets)

    return Policy(steps=steps, targets=targets)

    def test_generated_workflow_is_valid_yaml(self) -> None:

class TestCli(unittest.TestCase):
    """Command-line interface checks."""

    def test_check_passes_when_workflow_is_in_sync(self) -> None:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["python3", "scripts/generate_ci.py", "--check"],
            cwd=REPO_ROOT,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            check=False,
        )
        self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 0, result.stderr)

    def test_check_fails_when_workflow_is_out_of_sync(self) -> None:
        workflow = WORKFLOW_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        try:
            WORKFLOW_PATH.write_text(workflow + "\n# drift\n", encoding="utf-8")
            result = subprocess.run(
                ["python3", "scripts/generate_ci.py", "--check"],
                cwd=REPO_ROOT,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
                check=False,
            )
            self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 1)
            self.assertIn("out of sync", result.stderr)
        finally:
            WORKFLOW_PATH.write_text(workflow, encoding="utf-8")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

+            capture_output=True,
+            text=True,
+            check=False,
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 0, result.stderr)
+
+    def test_check_fails_when_workflow_is_out_of_sync(self) -> None:
+        workflow = WORKFLOW_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+        try:
+            WORKFLOW_PATH.write_text(workflow + "\n# drift\n", encoding="utf-8")
+            result = subprocess.run(
+                ["python3", "scripts/generate_ci.py", "--check"],
+                cwd=REPO_ROOT,
+                capture_output=True,
+                text=True,
+                check=False,
+            )
+            self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 1)
+            self.assertIn("out of sync", result.stderr)
+        finally:
+            WORKFLOW_PATH.write_text(workflow, encoding="utf-8")
+
+
 if __name__ == "__main__":
     unittest.main()

display.text:
name: Docs Pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: pages
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  build:
    name: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - uses: actions/configure-pages@45bfe0192ca1faeb007ade9deae92b16b8254a0d # v6.0.0
      - run: mise run //docs:build
      - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@fc324d3547104276b827a68afc52ff2a11cc49c9 # v5.0.0
        with:
          path: docs/dist

  deploy:
    name: deploy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    timeout-minutes: 10
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@cd2ce8fcbc39b97be8ca5fce6e763baed58fa128 # v5.0.0

1: # AGENTS.md
2: 
3: This project is a C API for MapLibre Native, built for low-level language
4: bindings and host integrations that need a C boundary instead of direct C++
5: interop or the popular MapLibre Android/iOS SDKs.
6: 
7: ## Project map
8: 
9: - `include/` — Public C API headers (the stable ABI surface).
10: - `src/` — C++ implementation behind the C headers, plus render backend adapters
11:   (Vulkan, Metal, OpenGL) and the Zig test support shim.
12: - `bindings/` — Language bindings (Zig, Rust, Java FFM, Java JNI) that wrap the
13:   C API in idiomatic target-language interfaces.
14: - `examples/` — Small demo apps per language/backend (`zig-map`, `rust-map`,
15:   `zig-readback`, `lwjgl-map`, `swift-map`).
16: - `third_party/` — Vendored dependencies, primarily the MapLibre Native git
17:   submodule.
18: - `docs/` — Astro/Starlight documentation site and generated API reference.
19: 
20: ## Workflow
21: 
22: ```bash
23: # Install/refresh all tools, submodules, and dependencies
24: mise install
25: 
26: # List available tasks across the workspace
27: mise tasks --all
28: 
29: # Build the native library (also runs configure)
30: mise run build
31: 
32: # Build and run C API + Zig binding tests (also runs build)
33: mise run test
34: 
35: # Build and run Rust binding tests (also runs build)
36: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
37: 
38: # Headless smoke test — no display needed
39: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run
40: 
41: # GUI map app — use a brief timeout or run in background
42: mise run //examples/zig-map:run
43: 
44: # Build and test for a different variant (override auto-detected env)
45: mise -E linux-x64-egl run test
46: 
47: # Run formatters and linters on _all_ files (will stage affected files)
48: mise run fix
49: 
50: # Run formatters and linters on targeted files (will stage affected files)
51: hk fix [FILES...]
52: ```
53: 
54: Available mise envs: `linux-x64-vulkan`, `linux-x64-egl`, `linux-arm64-vulkan`,
55: `linux-arm64-egl`, `macos-arm64-metal`, `macos-arm64-vulkan`, `macos-arm64-egl`,
56: `windows-x64-vulkan`, `windows-x64-wgl`. The host-matching variant is selected
57: automatically via `.miserc.toml`.
58: 
59: Formatters and linters run automatically on pre-commit; you usually don't need
60: to run them manually.
61: 
62: The environment is managed by mise. If you need to run a command that's not
63: already a mise task, use `mise exec -- ...` so repository tools and dependency
64: paths are available.
65: 
66: ## Pull requests
67: 
68: When you open a pull request, follow the repository PR template and write
69: **Summary** and **Test plan** in at most one sentence each. The user will expand
70: the PR description if more detail is needed. More context:
71: [AI_POLICY.md](./AI_POLICY.md).
72: 
73: ## Project Invariants
74: 
75: ### General
76: 
77: - Campsite rules apply: leave anything you touch tidier than when you found it.
78: - The environment is defined by `mise`; pixi is the current desktop native
79:   library provider behind mise-managed paths.
80: - The bindings are meant to be low level and broadly analogous to each other and
81:   to the C API, exposing MapLibre concepts directly, while following language
82:   conventions for memory and thread safety. Prioritize safety, similarity, and
83:   idioms, in that order.
84: - Bindings expose MapLibre Native concepts directly. Add redundant APIs or
85:   convenience helpers only when they are strongly justified by target-language
86:   safety or ergonomics.
87: - Bindings do not reimplement native validation; they validate binding-owned API
88:   shape, state, lifetimes, and memory safety.
89: - We're currently in a prerelease state, so breaking API changes are allowed and
90:   encouraged over leaving backwards compatibility shims.
91: 
92: ### Prose
93: 
94: Use positive wording for guidance. Use negative wording for real prohibitions,
95: safety rules, and hard boundaries.
96: 
97: - Prefer: "Examples stay small and focused."
98: - Avoid: "Examples should not grow into full applications."
99: - Prefer: "Higher-level adapters may add execution models above this layer."
100: - Avoid: "This layer should not try to manage execution models for every
101:   possible host."
102: 
103: ### Specifications
104: 
105: For specification writing:
106: 
107: - Standalone and testable: each requirement should be checkable on its own,
108:   without pointing at an example or the current tree.
109: - Add, don’t restate or hedge: link to other docs instead of copying them; avoid
110:   “or equivalent”, unnamed MAYs, and vague outcomes.
111: - Scope by constraint: family-wide sections state behavior; platform- or
112:   API-specific rules belong in clearly labeled subsections.
113: 
114: ### Testing
115: 
116: - The bindings tests include broad integration coverage for the C/C++ layer on
117:   targets where they run.
118: - For tests that _must_ reach below the bindings, there are dedicated Zig tests
119:   in src/c_api/tests.
120: - Each binding's test suite should stand on its own for the C API domains and
121:   targets it supports, using public binding APIs to validate both native
122:   workflows and binding-owned safety behavior.
123: - Avoid trivial tests, tests that verify constants, tests that assert a negative
124:   (unless valuable), tests that simply test third party code; we want to keep
125:   our test suite robust and high-value.
126: - Example apps don't need tests.
127: - Every test skip should be strictly justified. We do not skip rendering tests
128:   because the CI environment doesn't support them; we fix the environment.
129: 
130: ## Project Docs
131: 
132: Read these docs before changing related code:
133: 
134: - [Binding Specification](docs/src/content/docs/development/binding-specification.md)
135:   for binding requirements and language binding changes.
136: - [Map Example Specification](docs/src/content/docs/development/map-example-specification.md)
137:   for example requirements.
138: - [Overview](docs/src/content/docs/development/overview.md) for project layout,
139:   workflow, and tooling.
140: - [Concepts](docs/src/content/docs/concepts.md) for project scope, ownership,
141:   threading, events, rendering targets, and host integration boundaries.
142: - [C API Conventions](docs/src/content/docs/development/c-conventions.md) before
143:   changing public C headers, C ABI behavior, callbacks, diagnostics, or render
144:   target contracts.
145: 
146: ## External Docs
147: 
148: Read these docs whenever relevant:
149: 
150: - `mise`:
151:   - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration.html>
152:   - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration/settings.html>
153:   - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration/environments.html>

display.text:
# AGENTS.md

This project is a C API for MapLibre Native, built for low-level language
bindings and host integrations that need a C boundary instead of direct C++
interop or the popular MapLibre Android/iOS SDKs.

## Project map

- `include/` — Public C API headers (the stable ABI surface).
- `src/` — C++ implementation behind the C headers, plus render backend adapters
  (Vulkan, Metal, OpenGL) and the Zig test support shim.
- `bindings/` — Language bindings (Zig, Rust, Java FFM, Java JNI) that wrap the
  C API in idiomatic target-language interfaces.
- `examples/` — Small demo apps per language/backend (`zig-map`, `rust-map`,
  `zig-readback`, `lwjgl-map`, `swift-map`).
- `third_party/` — Vendored dependencies, primarily the MapLibre Native git
  submodule.
- `docs/` — Astro/Starlight documentation site and generated API reference.

## Workflow

```bash
# Install/refresh all tools, submodules, and dependencies
mise install

# List available tasks across the workspace
mise tasks --all

# Build the native library (also runs configure)
mise run build

# Build and run C API + Zig binding tests (also runs build)
mise run test

# Build and run Rust binding tests (also runs build)
mise run //bindings/rust:ci

# Headless smoke test — no display needed
mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

# GUI map app — use a brief timeout or run in background
mise run //examples/zig-map:run

# Build and test for a different variant (override auto-detected env)
mise -E linux-x64-egl run test

# Run formatters and linters on _all_ files (will stage affected files)
mise run fix

# Run formatters and linters on targeted files (will stage affected files)
hk fix [FILES...]
```

Available mise envs: `linux-x64-vulkan`, `linux-x64-egl`, `linux-arm64-vulkan`,
`linux-arm64-egl`, `macos-arm64-metal`, `macos-arm64-vulkan`, `macos-arm64-egl`,
`windows-x64-vulkan`, `windows-x64-wgl`. The host-matching variant is selected
automatically via `.miserc.toml`.

Formatters and linters run automatically on pre-commit; you usually don't need
to run them manually.

The environment is managed by mise. If you need to run a command that's not
already a mise task, use `mise exec -- ...` so repository tools and dependency
paths are available.

## Pull requests

When you open a pull request, follow the repository PR template and write
**Summary** and **Test plan** in at most one sentence each. The user will expand
the PR description if more detail is needed. More context:
[AI_POLICY.md](./AI_POLICY.md).

## Project Invariants

### General

- Campsite rules apply: leave anything you touch tidier than when you found it.
- The environment is defined by `mise`; pixi is the current desktop native
  library provider behind mise-managed paths.
- The bindings are meant to be low level and broadly analogous to each other and
  to the C API, exposing MapLibre concepts directly, while following language
  conventions for memory and thread safety. Prioritize safety, similarity, and
  idioms, in that order.
- Bindings expose MapLibre Native concepts directly. Add redundant APIs or
  convenience helpers only when they are strongly justified by target-language
  safety or ergonomics.
- Bindings do not reimplement native validation; they validate binding-owned API
  shape, state, lifetimes, and memory safety.
- We're currently in a prerelease state, so breaking API changes are allowed and
  encouraged over leaving backwards compatibility shims.

### Prose

Use positive wording for guidance. Use negative wording for real prohibitions,
safety rules, and hard boundaries.

- Prefer: "Examples stay small and focused."
- Avoid: "Examples should not grow into full applications."
- Prefer: "Higher-level adapters may add execution models above this layer."
- Avoid: "This layer should not try to manage execution models for every
  possible host."

### Specifications

For specification writing:

- Standalone and testable: each requirement should be checkable on its own,
  without pointing at an example or the current tree.
- Add, don’t restate or hedge: link to other docs instead of copying them; avoid
  “or equivalent”, unnamed MAYs, and vague outcomes.
- Scope by constraint: family-wide sections state behavior; platform- or
  API-specific rules belong in clearly labeled subsections.

### Testing

- The bindings tests include broad integration coverage for the C/C++ layer on
  targets where they run.
- For tests that _must_ reach below the bindings, there are dedicated Zig tests
  in src/c_api/tests.
- Each binding's test suite should stand on its own for the C API domains and
  targets it supports, using public binding APIs to validate both native
  workflows and binding-owned safety behavior.
- Avoid trivial tests, tests that verify constants, tests that assert a negative
  (unless valuable), tests that simply test third party code; we want to keep
  our test suite robust and high-value.
- Example apps don't need tests.
- Every test skip should be strictly justified. We do not skip rendering tests
  because the CI environment doesn't support them; we fix the environment.

## Project Docs

Read these docs before changing related code:

- [Binding Specification](docs/src/content/docs/development/binding-specification.md)
  for binding requirements and language binding changes.
- [Map Example Specification](docs/src/content/docs/development/map-example-specification.md)
  for example requirements.
- [Overview](docs/src/content/docs/development/overview.md) for project layout,
  workflow, and tooling.
- [Concepts](docs/src/content/docs/concepts.md) for project scope, ownership,
  threading, events, rendering targets, and host integration boundaries.
- [C API Conventions](docs/src/content/docs/development/c-conventions.md) before
  changing public C headers, C ABI behavior, callbacks, diagnostics, or render
  target contracts.

## External Docs

Read these docs whenever relevant:

- `mise`:
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration/settings.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/configuration/environments.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/dev-tools/>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/environments/>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/tasks/>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/tasks/toml-tasks.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/tasks/file-tasks.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/tasks/task-arguments.html>
  - <https://mise.jdx.dev/tasks/task-configuration.html>
  - ... and many more pages. Browse the site if needed.
- `hk`:
  - <https://hk.jdx.dev/configuration.html>
  - <https://hk.jdx.dev/builtins.html>
  - <https://hk.jdx.dev/reference/examples/>
  - <https://hk.jdx.dev/pkl_introduction.html>
- `dprint`:
  - <https://dprint.dev/config/>
  - <https://dprint.dev/plugins/>
- `vp` / Vite+:
  - <https://viteplus.dev/guide/>
  - <https://viteplus.dev/guide/monorepo>
  - <https://viteplus.dev/guide/lint>
  - <https://viteplus.dev/guide/run>
- `pixi`:
  - <https://pixi.prefix.dev/latest/reference/pixi_manifest/>
  - <https://pixi.prefix.dev/latest/reference/pixi_configuration/>

part.text:
The diff shows that we removed the comments explaining Kotlin/Native exclusions. The user said "Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy." The comments are not duplicated policy; they're explanatory. Should I preserve them?

The comments explained why certain targets are excluded from Kotlin/Native. Now the manifest has those explanations as comments per target. The generated workflow no longer has them. Is that OK?

The user wants to make the policy declarative and generate the workflow. The manifest is the source of truth. The workflow is generated. It's reasonable that explanatory comments live in the manifest, not the generated workflow. But maybe I should add a brief comment in the generated workflow pointing to the manifest for the rationale.

Alternatively, I could add a comment before the Kotlin/Native step summarizing the exclusions. But that would require the generator to know about specific capability rationale, which complicates it.

I think the cleanest is to leave the generated workflow without those comments, since the manifest has them. The generated header already points to the manifest.

But to be safe and preserve information, I could add the comments to the manifest more prominently. I already have them as inline comments in each target's capabilities array. That's good.

Actually, I realize the user said "Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy." The comments are behavior-adjacent documentation. I don't think removing them violates "preserve behavior". The behavior is the same.

However, I want to be thorough. Let me add a comment in the generated workflow's Kotlin/Native step pointing to the manifest. Actually, no - that adds complexity. The manifest is the source of truth.

OK, I'll leave it as is. But let me double-check that the manifest has clear comments for the exclusions. Yes, it does.

Now, another thing I notice: the diff removes a blank line before the Kotlin/Native step and before the LWJGL example step. The original had blank lines to group steps. My generated output doesn't have those blank lines. This is a minor formatting difference. Does it matter? Not for behavior. But it makes the workflow slightly less readable.

Should I preserve the blank lines? The user said preserve behavior. Formatting isn't behavior. But campsite rules say "leave anything you touch tidier". Removing blank lines makes it less tidy. I should add them back.

How to add blank lines in the generator? I can add blank lines between groups of steps in the `_render_steps` function. But grouping is policy knowledge. Or I can add a blank line before specific steps.

Actually, I can add a blank line before the first binding step (Java FFM) and before the first example step (LWJGL). This matches the original grouping:
- configure + build/test
- (blank)
- bindings
- (blank)
- examples

Let me update `_render_steps` to add blank lines. The simplest is to track the previous step's category and add a blank line when transitioning.

Categories:
- native: configure, build-native, test-native
- binding: java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, kotlin-native, rust, swift, zig
- example: lwjgl-example, rust-example, swift-example, zig-example, zig-readback

Actually, configure is always-run and build/test are native. The original had:
```
- run: configure
- name: Build native library
- name: Test native library

- name: Build Java FFM binding
...
- name: Test Zig binding

- name: Build LWJGL example
...
```

So blank lines between native and bindings, and between bindings and examples.

Let me implement this in `_render_steps`. I'll add a blank line when transitioning from native to binding, or from binding to example.

Actually, I can just check the step's capability. If it's "java-ffm" (first binding), add a blank line before it. If it's "lwjgl-example" (first example), add a blank line before it.

Let me add a more general approach: add a blank line before any step whose capability starts a new logical group. But that's arbitrary. Better: add blank lines based on step prefixes or explicit markers.

Simplest: add blank line before "java-ffm" and "lwjgl-example" specifically.

Actually, even simpler: the original blank lines separated:
1. Native steps
2. Binding steps
3. Example steps

I can categorize each step by checking if its task contains `//bindings/` or `//examples/`. For configure/build/test, they're native. Then add blank lines on category transitions.

Let me implement that.

zig_readback: true
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ !matrix.test_native }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ matrix.test_native }}
        run: mise run test

      - name: Build Java FFM binding
        if: ${{ matrix.java_ffm }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build
      - name: Build Java JNI binding
        if: ${{ matrix.java_jni }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-jni:build
      - name: Test .NET binding
        if: ${{ matrix.dotnet }}
        run: mise run //bindings/dotnet:test
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
        if: ${{ matrix.kotlin_native }}
        run: mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build
      - name: Test Rust binding
        if: ${{ matrix.rust }}
        run: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
      - name: Test Swift binding
        if: ${{ matrix.swift }}
        run: mise run //bindings/swift:test
      - name: Test Zig binding
        if: ${{ matrix.zig }}
        run: mise run //bindings/zig:ci

      - name: Build LWJGL example
        if: ${{ matrix.lwjgl_example }}
        run: mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build
      - name: Test Rust example
        if: ${{ matrix.rust_example }}
        run: mise run //examples/rust-map:ci
      - name: Build Swift example
        if: ${{ matrix.swift_example }}
        run: mise run //examples/swift-map:build
      - name: Build Zig example
        if: ${{ matrix.zig_example }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-map:build
      - name: Run Zig readback example
        if: ${{ matrix.zig_readback }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5

+## CI Target Support Policy
+
+The CI variant matrix is declared in `.github/targets.toml` and generated into
+`.github/workflows/ci.yml`. This keeps the target support policy in one
+place and prevents the checked-in workflow from drifting.
+
+When you change the supported targets, runners, or per-target tests, edit the
+manifest and regenerate the workflow:
+
+```bash
+mise run ci:generate
+```
+
+`mise run ci:lint` enforces that the generated workflow stays in sync with the
+manifest, and `mise run ci:test-generator` runs the generator's own tests.
+

part.text:
The diffs look good. Now let me check if the `scripts/__init__.py` and `scripts/tests/__init__.py` are needed. They're empty. For `unittest discover` to work with package imports, yes they are needed. But maybe I could avoid making `scripts` a package and instead run tests differently. The `ci:test-generator` task uses `python3 -m unittest discover -s scripts/tests`. With the `__init__.py` files, this works.

Actually, `unittest discover` with `-s scripts/tests` finds tests in that directory. The `__init__.py` in `scripts/tests` makes it a package. But the import `from scripts.generate_ci import ...` requires `scripts` to be a package. So `scripts/__init__.py` is needed.

I could avoid making `scripts` a package by using a relative import or by adjusting sys.path. But making it a package is fine.

Actually, wait - `scripts` directory already existed with `run-with-timeout.ps1`. Adding `__init__.py` makes it a Python package. That's fine.

Let me also check if there are any issues with the Python code style. The project uses ruff with line length 88. Let me check if my code fits. It should, but I haven't run ruff.

Since ruff isn't available, I can manually check line lengths. Let me look at the longest lines.

part.text:
Line 108 in generate_ci.py is 92 chars. Line 203 is in the f-string (a long GitHub Actions expression, but it's a single line in the output). Line 253 is also a long expression in the output.

The ruff config has line length 88, but it's for Python code formatting. The f-string content is YAML output, not Python code. However, ruff might still flag the Python source line length. Line 108 is Python code. Lines 203 and 253 are within a triple-quoted f-string, so they're content, not Python syntax.

But ruff does check line length of string contents too. Hmm. Actually, ruff's E501 checks line length including string contents. So if the f-string contains a 138-character line, ruff will flag it.

But this is YAML output. We want the YAML to have the expression on one line. We can either:
1. Disable E501 for these lines with `# noqa: E501`
2. Break the YAML expression across multiple lines
3. Accept that the generator file has long lines

Option 1 is clean. Let me add `# noqa: E501` to the relevant lines in the generator.

Wait, but the output YAML itself is checked by actionlint and dprint. The long lines in YAML are fine. We just need to suppress ruff for the Python source.

Let me add `# noqa: E501` to lines 108, 203, and 253. Actually, line 108 is in `_validate_policy`, a normal Python line. I can break it.

Let me fix line 108 by breaking it. And add noqa to the f-string lines.

Actually, line 108:
```python
                f"target {target.name!r} references unknown capabilities: {sorted(unknown)}"
```

I can break it:
```python
                f"target {target.name!r} references unknown capabilities: "
                f"{sorted(unknown)}"
```

Lines 203 and 253 are in the f-string. I'll add `# noqa: E501` at the end. But can you add comments inside f-strings? In Python, comments inside triple-quoted strings are literal text, not comments. So I can't add `# noqa` inside the f-string.

Alternative: disable ruff for the whole function or file. Or, break the GitHub Actions expression in the YAML output. But the expression should be on one line in YAML.

Actually, I can put the f-string lines outside the 88-char limit and add a `# noqa: E501` comment after the closing `"""`? No, that wouldn't apply to the lines inside.

Another approach: build those long strings in variables before the f-string, then reference them. Then the long lines are in normal string literals, and I can add `# noqa: E501`.

Or, I can use string concatenation with parentheses:
```python
    concurrency_expr = (
        "${{{{ github.workflow }}}}-${{{{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}}}"
    )  # noqa: E501
```

Then in the f-string: `group: {concurrency_expr}`.

Similarly for the long required job expression.

But this adds complexity. Alternatively, I can just add `# noqa: E501` at the top of the file with a ruff directive.

Actually, the simplest is to add `# ruff: noqa: E501` at the top of the file. But that disables line length checks for the whole file, which is not ideal.

Better: use `noqa` on specific lines that are long. For lines inside the f-string, I can break the f-string around those lines and put the noqa on the f-string part.

Wait, here's an idea: I can write the f-string such that the long expression line is constructed from a variable that I define with noqa.

```python
    concurrency_group = "${{{{ github.workflow }}}}-${{{{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}}}"  # noqa: E501
    ...
    return f"""...
concurrency:
  group: {concurrency_group}
...
"""
```

This keeps the f-string line short. The variable definition has the long line with noqa.

For the required job expression:
```python
    required_fail_expr = "${{{{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}}}"  # noqa: E501
```

Then use it in the f-string.

Let me refactor the render function to use variables for long expressions.

Actually, I realize the 138-char line in the f-string is inside the output. Even if I put it in a variable, the variable line is still 138 chars. With noqa, ruff ignores it. That's fine.

Let me refactor.

def render(policy: Policy) -> str:
    """Render the complete workflow YAML from the policy."""
    matrix = _render_matrix(policy)
    steps = _render_steps(policy)

    # Long GitHub Actions expressions are assigned to variables so the f-string
    # stays readable and ruff's line-length check can be suppressed locally.
    concurrency_group = (
        "${{{{ github.workflow }}}}-${{{{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}}}"  # noqa: E501
    )
    cancel_in_progress = "${{{{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}}}"
    always = "${{{{ always() }}}}"
    required_failed = "${{{{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}}}"  # noqa: E501
    to_json = "${{{{ toJSON(needs) }}}}"

    return f"""# Generated from .github/targets.toml by `mise run ci:generate`.
# Do not edit this file directly; edit the manifest and regenerate.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: {concurrency_group}
  cancel-in-progress: {cancel_in_progress}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@{ACTION_CHECKOUT} # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:test-generator
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@{ACTION_CHECKOUT} # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{{{ matrix.mise_env }}}}
    runs-on: ${{{{ matrix.runner }}}}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{{{ matrix.mise_env }}}}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
{matrix}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@{ACTION_CHECKOUT} # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

{steps}

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: {always}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: {required_failed}
        run: |
          echo '{to_json}'
          exit 1

      - run: true
"""

part.text:
Wait, the output shows `${{{{ github.workflow }}}}` again! That's wrong. It should be `${{ github.workflow }}`.

What happened? I extracted `concurrency_group = "${{{{ github.workflow }}}}-${{{{ ... }}}}"` with four braces because in a normal Python string (not f-string), `{{` is literal `{`. Wait, no! In a normal Python string, `{{` is just two literal `{` characters. There's no escaping in normal strings. So `"${{{{ github.workflow }}}}"` is literally `${{{{ github.workflow }}}}` (with four braces). I mistakenly used f-string escaping in a normal string.

Let me fix this. In a normal string, to output `${{`, I just write `${{`. So:

```python
concurrency_group = "${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}"
```

Similarly:
```python
cancel_in_progress = "${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}"
always = "${{ always() }}"
required_failed = "${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}"
to_json = "${{ toJSON(needs) }}"
```

Then in the f-string, `{concurrency_group}` will insert the string with `{{`. In the f-string, `{{` is interpreted as escaped `{` and outputs `{`. Wait no!

If the variable value is `"${{ github.workflow }}"` (literal two braces), and I use `{concurrency_group}` in an f-string, the f-string will output the literal value of the variable, which is `${{ github.workflow }}`. F-string variable substitution doesn't process `{` in the variable value. It just inserts the string as-is.

Wait, is that true? Let me verify.

part.text:
Yes! Variable substitution in f-strings inserts the literal value. So I should use normal strings with `${{` and then reference them in the f-string. The f-string will output the literal `{{` from the variable value.

So I need to change the extracted variables to use normal `${{` syntax, not escaped `{{{{`. Let me fix.

    # stays readable and ruff's line-length check can be suppressed locally.
    concurrency_group = (

    )
    cancel_in_progress = "${{{{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}}}"

    required_failed = "${{{{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}}}"  # noqa: E501
    to_json = "${{{{ toJSON(needs) }}}}"

metadata.preview:
# CI target support policy.
# This file is the single source of truth for the variant matrix in
# `.github/workflows/ci.yml`. Edit it, then regenerate the workflow with:
#
#   mise run ci:generate
#
# CI enforces that the checked-in workflow stays in sync with this manifest.

# Steps are rendered in order under the `variants` job. Each step either always
# runs, runs only on build-only targets, runs only on test targets, or runs when
# the target declares the named capability.
[[step]]
name = "Configure native library"
task = "mise run configure"
always = true

[[step]]
name = "Build native library"
task = "mise run build"
build_only = true

display.text:
# CI target support policy.
# This file is the single source of truth for the variant matrix in
# `.github/workflows/ci.yml`. Edit it, then regenerate the workflow with:
#
#   mise run ci:generate
#
# CI enforces that the checked-in workflow stays in sync with this manifest.

# Steps are rendered in order under the `variants` job. Each step either always
# runs, runs only on build-only targets, runs only on test targets, or runs when
# the target declares the named capability.
[[step]]
name = "Configure native library"
task = "mise run configure"
always = true

[[step]]
name = "Build native library"
task = "mise run build"
build_only = true

[[step]]
name = "Test native library"
task = "mise run test"
test_only = true

[[step]]
name = "Build Java FFM binding"
task = "mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build"
capability = "java-ffm"

[[step]]

task = "mise run //bindings/java-jni:build"
capability = "java-jni"

capability = "dotnet"

[[step]]

capability = "kotlin-native"

[[step]]

task = "mise run //bindings/rust:ci"
capability = "rust"

[[step]]
name = "Test Swift binding"
task = "mise run //bindings/swift:test"
capability = "swift"

[[step]]

task = "mise run //bindings/zig:ci"
capability = "zig"

[[step]]

capability = "rust-example"

state.title: .github/targets.toml

part.text: The manifest looks good. Let me also verify the generator script one more time for any issues.

state.output:
<path>/app/scripts/generate_ci.py</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: """Generate `.github/workflows/ci.yml` from `.github/targets.toml`.
3: 
4: The manifest declares the supported CI targets and the capabilities each target
5: runs. This script renders the checked-in workflow directly so the policy has a
6: single source of truth and stays in sync.
7: """
8: 
9: from __future__ import annotations
10: 
11: import argparse
12: import sys
13: import tomllib
14: from dataclasses import dataclass
15: from pathlib import Path
16: 
17: 
18: @dataclass(frozen=True)
19: class Step:
20:     """A step in the variants job."""
21: 
22:     name: str
23:     task: str
24:     always: bool = False
25:     build_only: bool = False
26:     test_only: bool = False
27:     capability: str | None = None
28: 
29: 
30: @dataclass(frozen=True)
31: class Target:
32:     """A supported CI target variant."""
33: 
34:     name: str
35:     runner: str
36:     build_only: bool
37:     capabilities: frozenset[str]
38:     notes: str | None = None
39: 
40: 
41: @dataclass(frozen=True)
42: class Policy:
43:     """The complete CI target support policy."""
44: 
45:     steps: list[Step]
46:     targets: list[Target]
47: 
48: 
49: # Pinned action hashes used in the generated workflow. Keep these in sync with
50: # repository-wide updates (e.g. `mise run actions-up`).
51: ACTION_CHECKOUT = "df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10"  # v6.0.3
52: 
53: 
54: def load_policy(path: Path) -> Policy:
55:     """Parse the target policy manifest."""
56:     with path.open("rb") as file:
57:         data = tomllib.load(file)
58: 
59:     steps = [
60:         Step(
61:             name=str(step["name"]),
62:             task=str(step["task"]),
63:             always=bool(step.get("always", False)),
64:             build_only=bool(step.get("build_only", False)),
65:             test_only=bool(step.get("test_only", False)),
66:             capability=str(step["capability"]) if step.get("capability") else None,
67:         )
68:         for step in data.get("step", [])
69:     ]
70:     targets = [
71:         Target(
72:             name=str(target["name"]),
73:             runner=str(target["runner"]),
74:             build_only=bool(target["build_only"]),
75:             capabilities=frozenset(str(c) for c in target.get("capabilities", [])),
76:             notes=str(target["notes"]) if target.get("notes") else None,
77:         )
78:         for target in data.get("target", [])
79:     ]
80: 
81:     _validate_policy(steps, targets)
82: 
83:     return Policy(steps=steps, targets=targets)
84: 
85: 
86: def _validate_policy(steps: list[Step], targets: list[Target]) -> None:
87:     """Fail fast on manifest mistakes that would produce broken workflows."""
88:     known_capabilities: set[str] = set()
89:     for step in steps:
90:         if step.capability:
91:             known_capabilities.add(step.capability)
92:         flags = [
93:             step.always,
94:             step.build_only,
95:             step.test_only,
96:             step.capability is not None,
97:         ]
98:         if sum(flags) != 1:
99:             raise ValueError(
100:                 f"step {step.name!r} must set exactly one of "
101:                 "always, build_only, test_only, or capability"
102:             )
103: 
104:     for target in targets:
105:         unknown = target.capabilities - known_capabilities
106:         if unknown:
107:             raise ValueError(
108:                 f"target {target.name!r} references unknown capabilities: "
109:                 f"{sorted(unknown)}"
110:             )
111: 
112: 
113: def matrix_key(capability: str) -> str:
114:     """Return a valid GitHub Actions matrix key for a capability name."""
115:     return capability.replace("-", "_")
116: 
117: 
118: def _render_matrix(policy: Policy) -> str:
119:     """Render the `strategy.matrix.include` block for the variants job."""
120:     lines = ["        include:"]
121:     for target in policy.targets:
122:         lines.append(f"          - mise_env: {target.name}")
123:         lines.append(f"            runner: {target.runner}")
124:         lines.append(f"            test_native: {str(not target.build_only).lower()}")
125:         for step in policy.steps:
126:             if step.capability:
127:                 flag = matrix_key(step.capability)
128:                 enabled = str(step.capability in target.capabilities).lower()
129:                 lines.append(f"            {flag}: {enabled}")
130:     return "\n".join(lines)
131: 
132: 
133: def _step_category(step: Step) -> str:
134:     """Return a broad category used to group steps in the rendered workflow."""
135:     if step.always or step.build_only or step.test_only:
136:         return "native"
137:     if step.task.startswith("mise run //bindings/"):
138:         return "binding"
139:     if step.task.startswith("mise run //examples/"):
140:         return "example"
141:     return "other"
142: 
143: 
144: def _render_steps(policy: Policy) -> str:
145:     """Render the steps for the variants job."""
146:     lines: list[str] = []
147:     previous_category = ""
148:     for step in policy.steps:
149:         category = _step_category(step)
150:         if previous_category and category != previous_category:
151:             lines.append("")
152:         previous_category = category
153: 
154:         if step.always:
155:             lines.append(f"      - run: {step.task}")
156:         elif step.build_only:
157:             lines.extend(
158:                 [
159:                     f"      - name: {step.name}",
160:                     "        if: ${{ !matrix.test_native }}",
161:                     f"        run: {step.task}",
162:                 ]
163:             )
164:         elif step.test_only:
165:             lines.extend(
166:                 [
167:                     f"      - name: {step.name}",
168:                     "        if: ${{ matrix.test_native }}",
169:                     f"        run: {step.task}",
170:                 ]
171:             )
172:         elif step.capability:
173:             flag = matrix_key(step.capability)
174:             lines.extend(
175:                 [
176:                     f"      - name: {step.name}",
177:                     f"        if: ${{{{ matrix.{flag} }}}}",
178:                     f"        run: {step.task}",
179:                 ]
180:             )
181:     return "\n".join(lines)
182: 
183: 
184: def render(policy: Policy) -> str:
185:     """Render the complete workflow YAML from the policy."""
186:     matrix = _render_matrix(policy)
187:     steps = _render_steps(policy)
188: 
189:     # Long GitHub Actions expressions are assigned to variables so the f-string
190:     # stays readable and ruff's line-length check can be suppressed locally.
191:     concurrency_group = (
192:         "${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}"  # noqa: E501
193:     )
194:     cancel_in_progress = "${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}"
195:     always = "${{ always() }}"
196:     required_failed = "${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}"  # noqa: E501
197:     to_json = "${{ toJSON(needs) }}"
198: 
199:     return f"""# Generated from .github/targets.toml by `mise run ci:generate`.
200: # Do not edit this file directly; edit the manifest and regenerate.
201: 
202: name: CI
203: 
204: on:
205:   push:
206:     branches: [main]
207:   pull_request:
208:     branches: [main]
209: 
210: permissions:
211:   contents: read
212: 
213: concurrency:
214:   group: {concurrency_group}
215:   cancel-in-progress: {cancel_in_progress}
216: 
217: jobs:
218:   hygiene:
219:     name: hygiene
220:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
221:     timeout-minutes: 30
222:     steps:
223:       - uses: actions/checkout@{ACTION_CHECKOUT} # v6.0.3
224:       - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
225:       - run: mise run ci:test-generator
226:       - run: mise run ci:lint
227: 
228:   docs:
229:     name: docs
230:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
231:     timeout-minutes: 60
232:     steps:
233:       - uses: actions/checkout@{ACTION_CHECKOUT} # v6.0.3
234:       - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
235:       - run: mise run //docs:build
236: 
237:   variants:
238:     name: variant / ${{{{ matrix.mise_env }}}}
239:     runs-on: ${{{{ matrix.runner }}}}
240:     timeout-minutes: 120
241:     env:
242:       MISE_ENV: ${{{{ matrix.mise_env }}}}
243:     strategy:
244:       fail-fast: false
245:       matrix:
246: {matrix}
247:     steps:
248:       - uses: actions/checkout@{ACTION_CHECKOUT} # v6.0.3
249:       - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
250: 
251: {steps}
252: 
253:   required:
254:     name: ci-required
255:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
256:     timeout-minutes: 5
257:     if: {always}
258:     needs:
259:       - hygiene
260:       - docs
261:       - variants
262:     steps:
263:       - name: Fail if required jobs failed
264:         if: {required_failed}
265:         run: |
266:           echo '{to_json}'
267:           exit 1
268: 
269:       - run: true
270: """
271: 
272: 
273: def main() -> int:
274:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate `.github/workflows/ci.yml` from `.github/targets.toml`.

The manifest declares the supported CI targets and the capabilities each target
runs. This script renders the checked-in workflow directly so the policy has a
single source of truth and stays in sync.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import sys
import tomllib
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path


@dataclass(frozen=True)

    """A step in the variants job."""

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate `.github/workflows/ci.yml` from `.github/targets.toml`.

The manifest declares the supported CI targets and the capabilities each target
runs. This script renders the checked-in workflow directly so the policy has a
single source of truth and stays in sync.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import sys
import tomllib
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Step:
    """A step in the variants job."""

    name: str
    task: str
    always: bool = False
    build_only: bool = False
    test_only: bool = False
    capability: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Target:
    """A supported CI target variant."""

    name: str
    runner: str
    build_only: bool
    capabilities: frozenset[str]
    notes: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Policy:
    """The complete CI target support policy."""

    steps: list[Step]
    targets: list[Target]


# Pinned action hashes used in the generated workflow. Keep these in sync with
# repository-wide updates (e.g. `mise run actions-up`).
ACTION_CHECKOUT = "df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10"  # v6.0.3


def load_policy(path: Path) -> Policy:
    """Parse the target policy manifest."""
    with path.open("rb") as file:
        data = tomllib.load(file)

    steps = [
        Step(
            name=str(step["name"]),
            task=str(step["task"]),
            always=bool(step.get("always", False)),
            build_only=bool(step.get("build_only", False)),
            test_only=bool(step.get("test_only", False)),
            capability=str(step["capability"]) if step.get("capability") else None,
        )
        for step in data.get("step", [])
    ]
    targets = [
        Target(
            name=str(target["name"]),
            runner=str(target["runner"]),
            build_only=bool(target["build_only"]),
            capabilities=frozenset(str(c) for c in target.get("capabilities", [])),
            notes=str(target["notes"]) if target.get("notes") else None,
        )
        for target in data.get("target", [])
    ]

    _validate_policy(steps, targets)

    return Policy(steps=steps, targets=targets)


def _validate_policy(steps: list[Step], targets: list[Target]) -> None:
    """Fail fast on manifest mistakes that would produce broken workflows."""
    known_capabilities: set[str] = set()
    for step in steps:
        if step.capability:
            known_capabilities.add(step.capability)
        flags = [
            step.always,
            step.build_only,
            step.test_only,
            step.capability is not None,
        ]
        if sum(flags) != 1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"step {step.name!r} must set exactly one of "
                "always, build_only, test_only, or capability"
            )

    for target in targets:
        unknown = target.capabilities - known_capabilities
        if unknown:
            raise ValueError(
                f"target {target.name!r} references unknown capabilities: "
                f"{sorted(unknown)}"
            )


def matrix_key(capability: str) -> str:
    """Return a valid GitHub Actions matrix key for a capability name."""
    return capability.replace("-", "_")


def _render_matrix(policy: Policy) -> str:
    """Render the `strategy.matrix.include` block for the variants job."""
    lines = ["        include:"]
    for target in policy.targets:
        lines.append(f"          - mise_env: {target.name}")
        lines.append(f"            runner: {target.runner}")
        lines.append(f"            test_native: {str(not target.build_only).lower()}")
        for step in policy.steps:
            if step.capability:
                flag = matrix_key(step.capability)
                enabled = str(step.capability in target.capabilities).lower()
                lines.append(f"            {flag}: {enabled}")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _step_category(step: Step) -> str:
    """Return a broad category used to group steps in the rendered workflow."""
    if step.always or step.build_only or step.test_only:
        return "native"
    if step.task.startswith("mise run //bindings/"):
        return "binding"
    if step.task.startswith("mise run //examples/"):
        return "example"
    return "other"


def _render_steps(policy: Policy) -> str:
    """Render the steps for the variants job."""
    lines: list[str] = []
    previous_category = ""
    for step in policy.steps:
        category = _step_category(step)
        if previous_category and category != previous_category:
            lines.append("")
        previous_category = category

        if step.always:
            lines.append(f"      - run: {step.task}")
        elif step.build_only:
            lines.extend(
                [
                    f"      - name: {step.name}",
                    "        if: ${{ !matrix.test_native }}",
                    f"        run: {step.task}",
                ]
            )
        elif step.test_only:
            lines.extend(
                [
                    f"      - name: {step.name}",
                    "        if: ${{ matrix.test_native }}",
                    f"        run: {step.task}",
                ]
            )
        elif step.capability:
            flag = matrix_key(step.capability)
            lines.extend(
                [
                    f"      - name: {step.name}",
                    f"        if: ${{{{ matrix.{flag} }}}}",
                    f"        run: {step.task}",
                ]
            )
    return "\n".join(lines)


def render(policy: Policy) -> str:
    """Render the complete workflow YAML from the policy."""
    matrix = _render_matrix(policy)
    steps = _render_steps(policy)

    # Long GitHub Actions expressions are assigned to variables so the f-string
    # stays readable and ruff's line-length check can be suppressed locally.
    concurrency_group = (
        "${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}"  # noqa: E501
    )
    cancel_in_progress = "${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}"
    always = "${{ always() }}"
    required_failed = "${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}"  # noqa: E501
    to_json = "${{ toJSON(needs) }}"

    return f"""# Generated from .github/targets.toml by `mise run ci:generate`.
# Do not edit this file directly; edit the manifest and regenerate.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: {concurrency_group}
  cancel-in-progress: {cancel_in_progress}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@{ACTION_CHECKOUT} # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:test-generator
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@{ACTION_CHECKOUT} # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{{{ matrix.mise_env }}}}
    runs-on: ${{{{ matrix.runner }}}}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{{{ matrix.mise_env }}}}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
{matrix}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@{ACTION_CHECKOUT} # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

{steps}

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: {always}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: {required_failed}
        run: |
          echo '{to_json}'
          exit 1

      - run: true
"""


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate CI workflow from target policy manifest",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--manifest",
        type=Path,
        default=Path(".github/targets.toml"),
        help="Path to the target policy manifest (default: .github/targets.toml)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        type=Path,
        default=Path(".github/workflows/ci.yml"),
        help="Path to write the generated workflow (default: .github/workflows/ci.yml)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--check",
        action="store_true",
        help="Exit with a non-zero status if the output would change",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    policy = load_policy(args.manifest)
    generated = render(policy)

    if args.check:
        existing = args.output.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        if generated != existing:
            print(
                f"error: {args.output} is out of sync with {args.manifest}",
                file=sys.stderr,
            )
            print("run: mise run ci:generate", file=sys.stderr)
            return 1
    else:
        args.output.write_text(generated, encoding="utf-8")
        print(f"generated {args.output}")

    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

"""Tests for the CI workflow generator."""

from __future__ import annotations

import subprocess
import unittest
from pathlib import Path

from scripts.generate_ci import load_policy, matrix_key, render

try:
    import yaml as _yaml
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - tested in environments with PyYAML
    _yaml = None

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent
MANIFEST_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".github" / "targets.toml"
WORKFLOW_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "ci.yml"

display.text:
"""Tests for the CI workflow generator."""

from __future__ import annotations

import subprocess
import unittest
from pathlib import Path

from scripts.generate_ci import load_policy, matrix_key, render

try:
    import yaml as _yaml
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - tested in environments with PyYAML
    _yaml = None

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent
MANIFEST_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".github" / "targets.toml"
WORKFLOW_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "ci.yml"


class TestPolicy(unittest.TestCase):
    """Policy-level checks against the checked-in manifest."""

    def test_load_policy_parses_all_targets(self) -> None:
        policy = load_policy(MANIFEST_PATH)
        target_names = {target.name for target in policy.targets}
        self.assertEqual(
            target_names,
            {
                "linux-arm64-egl",
                "linux-arm64-vulkan",
                "linux-x64-egl",
                "linux-x64-vulkan",
                "macos-arm64-metal",
                "macos-arm64-vulkan",
                "macos-arm64-egl",
                "ios-arm64-metal",
                "ios-simulator-arm64-metal",
                "windows-x64-vulkan",
                "windows-x64-wgl",
            },
        )

    def test_ios_targets_are_build_only(self) -> None:
        policy = load_policy(MANIFEST_PATH)
        for target in policy.targets:
            with self.subTest(target=target.name):
                if target.name.startswith("ios-"):
                    self.assertTrue(target.build_only)
                    self.assertFalse(target.capabilities)

    def test_macos_arm64_egl_is_smoke_build(self) -> None:
        policy = load_policy(MANIFEST_PATH)
        target = next(t for t in policy.targets if t.name == "macos-arm64-egl")
        self.assertEqual(target.capabilities, {"zig", "zig-readback"})

    def test_kotlin_native_excluded_on_windows_and_linux_arm64(self) -> None:
        policy = load_policy(MANIFEST_PATH)
        for target in policy.targets:
            with self.subTest(target=target.name):
                if target.name.startswith(("windows-", "linux-arm64-")):
                    self.assertNotIn("kotlin-native", target.capabilities)
                elif not target.build_only and target.name != "macos-arm64-egl":
                    self.assertIn("kotlin-native", target.capabilities)

    def test_lwjgl_example_only_on_vulkan(self) -> None:
        policy = load_policy(MANIFEST_PATH)
        for target in policy.targets:
            with self.subTest(target=target.name):
                if "vulkan" in target.name:
                    self.assertIn("lwjgl-example", target.capabilities)
                else:
                    self.assertNotIn("lwjgl-example", target.capabilities)


class TestGenerator(unittest.TestCase):
    """Generator behavior and output checks."""

    def test_matrix_key_replaces_hyphens(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(matrix_key("java-ffm"), "java_ffm")
        self.assertEqual(matrix_key("kotlin-native"), "kotlin_native")
        self.assertEqual(matrix_key("zig-readback"), "zig_readback")

    def test_generated_workflow_matches_checked_in(self) -> None:
        policy = load_policy(MANIFEST_PATH)
        generated = render(policy)
        checked_in = WORKFLOW_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        self.assertEqual(
            generated,
            checked_in,
            f"{WORKFLOW_PATH.name} is out of sync with {MANIFEST_PATH.name}. "
            "Run: mise run ci:generate",
        )

    @unittest.skipIf(_yaml is None, "PyYAML not available")
    def test_generated_workflow_is_valid_yaml(self) -> None:
        policy = load_policy(MANIFEST_PATH)
        generated = render(policy)
        document = _yaml.safe_load(generated)
        self.assertEqual(document["name"], "CI")
        self.assertIn("variants", document["jobs"])
        self.assertIn("hygiene", document["jobs"])
        self.assertIn("required", document["jobs"])


class TestCli(unittest.TestCase):
    """Command-line interface checks."""

    def test_check_passes_when_workflow_is_in_sync(self) -> None:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["python3", "scripts/generate_ci.py", "--check"],
            cwd=REPO_ROOT,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            check=False,
        )
        self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 0, result.stderr)

    def test_check_fails_when_workflow_is_out_of_sync(self) -> None:
        workflow = WORKFLOW_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        try:
            WORKFLOW_PATH.write_text(workflow + "\n# drift\n", encoding="utf-8")
            result = subprocess.run(
                ["python3", "scripts/generate_ci.py", "--check"],
                cwd=REPO_ROOT,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
                check=False,
            )
            self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 1)
            self.assertIn("out of sync", result.stderr)
        finally:
            WORKFLOW_PATH.write_text(workflow, encoding="utf-8")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

Actually, I can make it cleaner by using a typed variable. But it's probably fine.

Now let me run the full test suite one more time.

display.text:
# Contributing

Discuss changes in `#maplibre` on the
[OSM-US Slack](https://slack.openstreetmap.us/).

## Before Making Changes

See the
[development overview](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/development/)
for platform setup, pinned tools, local commands, tests, and examples.

Read [concepts](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/concepts/) before
changing behavior. Read the
[C API Conventions](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/development/c-conventions/)
before changing public C interfaces or C ABI behavior. Read the
[Binding specification](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/development/binding-specification/)
before changing language bindings or generated binding reference docs.

Keep pull requests focused on one reviewable change. The reviewer should be able
to connect the use case, public behavior, implementation, and validation without
separating unrelated work.

## Pull Requests

Open a pull request when the change is ready for review and include:

- the problem or use case;
- the public API or behavior change, if any;
- the validation you ran;
- platform limitations or native MapLibre behavior you checked;

If you use AI assistance, follow the [AI policy](./AI_POLICY.md).

display.text: # MapLibre Native FFI [![MapLibre](https://img.shields.io/badge/MapLibre-396CB2)](https://maplibre.org/) [![Slack](https://img.shields.io/badge/Slack-4A154B?logo=slack&logoColor=white)](https://slack.openstreetmap.us/) [![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/sargunv/maplibre-native-ffi?label=License)](./LICENSE) This project provides an experimental C API for [MapLibre Native](https://github.com/maplibre/maplibre-native). It is built for low-level language bindings and host integrations that need a C boundary instead of direct C++ interop. The API exposes MapLibre Native concepts directly. Framework concerns such as gestures, widgets, declarative UI, and application lifecycle integration belong in downstream adapters. MapLibre Native FFI is pre-1.0. The C ABI is unstable while `mln_c_version()` returns `0`. ## Documentation Read the documentation site for concepts, usage guides, the generated C API reference, and contributor notes. - [Concepts](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/concepts/) - [Usage guides](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/guides/installation/) - [Reference](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/reference/c/) - [Development overview](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/development/overview/) - [Binding specification](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/development/binding-specification/) ## Status The C API covers most of the MapLibre Native API surface. Advanced extension APIs, including custom layers and shader registry access, are not yet included. Platform support is still filling in -- see the [Overview](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/#status) page for full platform status. ## License BSD 3-Clause. See [LICENSE](LICENSE). Copyright (c) 2026 MapLibre contributors.

part.text: Done. Let me provide a concise summary to the user.

Done. I made the CI target support policy declarative and generated the workflow from a manifest.

**What changed:**
- Added `.github/targets.toml` — the single source of truth for CI targets, runners, and per-target capabilities.
- Added `scripts/generate_ci.py` — a stdlib-only generator that renders `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.
- Added `scripts/tests/test_generate_ci.py` — 10 unit tests covering policy parsing, capability rules, YAML validity, and sync enforcement.
- Regenerated `.github/workflows/ci.yml` from the manifest. The old duplicated `if:` conditions are now per-target matrix flags (e.g. `matrix.kotlin_native`, `matrix.lwjgl_example`).
- Added mise tasks: `ci:generate`, `ci:check-sync`, `ci:test-generator`.
- Wired `ci:check-sync` into `mise run ci:lint` so CI and pre-commit enforce that the workflow stays in sync.
- Updated `AGENTS.md` and `docs/src/content/docs/development/overview.md`.

**Preserved behavior:** all 11 variants, runners, native build/test gating, binding/example inclusion rules, and the required-job aggregation are unchanged. The only structural change is replacing repeated string conditions with declarative matrix booleans.

**Verification:**
- `python3 -m unittest discover -s scripts/tests -v` passes.
- `python3 scripts/generate_ci.py --check` passes.
- Committed as `9ced503`.
